sourceRows.Summary: Mass splittings arise from nontrivial compactification/twist data rather than from inserting local mass terms by hand.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Direct geometric/topological mass-generation lead; still needs GU-local compactification map, twist source, and physical projection.

sourceRows.Summary: Classifies stable configurations of extra-dimensional gauge theories compactified on a torus with Scherk-Schwarz boundary data.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Boundary/twist geometry lead; does not derive W/Z/H source rows from GU.

sourceRows.Summary: Uses extra-dimensional electroweak symmetry breaking with a predicted Higgs mass around 200 GeV in the model considered.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Phenomenological electroweak lead, but observed-Higgs compatibility and target-independent GU scale lineage are missing.

sourceRows.Summary: Identifies Higgs fields with internal gauge-field components and relates electroweak breaking to Wilson-line or Scherk-Schwarz twist data.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: A W mass can depend on a Wilson-line phase over compactification radius, but the phase/radius and top/Higgs sectors remain model data.

sourceRows.FindingKind: MSSM electroweak breaking from Scherk-Schwarz SUSY breaking
sourceRows.Summary: Scherk-Schwarz supersymmetry breaking can induce Higgs-sector soft terms and radiative electroweak breaking.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: External SUSY model-building route; still needs GU-local soft-term, RG, and observed-Higgs lineage.

sourceRows.FindingKind: Flux compactification with Wilson-line scalar condensate
sourceRows.Summary: Studies gauge symmetry breaking in flux compactification with Wilson-line scalar condensates.
sourceRows.PredictionImpact: Modern compactification/twist context, not a GU-local W/Z/H prediction source.

decision: Do not promote W/Z or Higgs physical masses from the Scherk-Schwarz/twisted-compactification route in this repository. The route is a genuine geometric mass-generation lead through compactification twists, Wilson-line phases, or boundary data, but the current evidence leaves the twist angle, compactification radius, electroweak embedding, weak angle, Higgs sector, and GeV normalization as external or model-dependent data rather than GU-local source-lineage rows.
nextRequiredArtifact: A GU-local theorem mapping Shiab/Upsilon/connection data to a compactification/twist/holonomy sector.
A target-independent source for the Scherk-Schwarz twist angle or Wilson-line phase and compactification radius.
Observed photon/W/Z/H projection rows with neutral mixing and weak-angle/gauge-coupling lineage.
A Higgs scalar-source or compatible gauge-Higgs replacement that accounts for the observed Higgs mass.
RG, threshold, chirality/fermion-sector, and GeV-unit normalization lineage validated through Phase201/Phase256.
